calendar.matters: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution

Off Calendar May-13-2026 Continued To Aug-19-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing On The Filing Of A Petition For Final Distribution Or A Petition For Approval Of Report Of Status Of Administration
Off Calendar Feb-18-2026 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing On The Filing Of A Petition For Final Distribution Or A Petition For Approval Of Report Of Status Of Administration
Off Calendar Jan-21-2026 Continued To Feb-18-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Trang La for administrator Anthony Bedoussac (via Zoom).

The matter was called for hearing on Status on Close of Estate.

The Court inquired as to how much time would be needed for counsel to file a Petition for Final Distribution. Ms. La stated that the petition could be filed by the end of the week.

Following discussion, the Court continued the matter to February 18, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, and indicated that if the Petition for Final Distribution is filed prior to that date, the status hearing will come off calendar.

Judge: Russell S. Roeca; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing On The Filing Of A Petition For Final Distribution Or A Petition For Approval Of Report Of Status Of Administration
Off Calendar Nov-18-2025 Continued To Jan-21-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jul-01-2025 Granted Per Written Order

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar May-14-2025 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Mar-05-2025 Continued To May-14-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Trang La for Anthony Bedoussac, Administrator (via Zoom).

The Court inquired about the status of the Inventory and Appraisal, noting that none had been filed, not even a partial inventory. Ms. La explained that delays were due to administrative challenges in collecting retirement funds and that she expects to collect the funds by the end of the week. She noted that the estate is small, consisting mainly of the retirement funds and a $5,000 bank account. Ms. La stated that she could file a partial inventory and appraisal by the end of the week.

Ms. La requested a two-month continuance to allow time to complete the process of collecting retirement funds. The court continued the matter to May 14, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)
